title: Chewy Bitter Saudi Date Dumplings
description: Soft, chewy dumplings filled with bittersweet Medjool dates, steamed to perfection and served warm for a comforting plantbased treat.
instructions: 1. In a large bowl whisk the all‑purpose flour, gluten‑free flour, baking powder and salt.
2. Add the oat milk and dark brown sugar, stirring until a soft dough forms; it should be slightly sticky but not wet.
3. Turn the dough onto a floured surface and knead for 2–3 minutes until smooth. Cover with a damp cloth and set aside for 10 minutes.
4. While the dough rests, combine the chopped dates, cardamom, and black salt in a small bowl. Mix until the dates are evenly coated.
5. Divide the rested dough into 12 equal portions. Roll each into a small ball, then flatten into a disc about 3 inches in diameter.
6. Place a teaspoonful of the date filling in the center of each disc, then fold the dough over and pinch the edges shut to seal, forming a dumpling.
7. Bring a large pot of water to a gentle boil and add a splash of sea salt. Carefully drop the dumplings in, stirring gently to prevent sticking.
8. Cook the dumplings for 5–7 minutes, or until they rise to the surface and are cooked through.
9. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain. Arrange on a plate and drizzle with a little extra oat milk if desired.
10. Sprinkle with chopped pistachios for added crunch and serve warm.
